question: Define a `Book` class with private data members `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Provide public getter methods `getTitle()`, `getAuthor()`, `getIsbn()`, and a `displayBookInfo()` method that prints all book details.

test_cases.input: Book("The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy", "Douglas Adams", "978-0345391803")
test_cases.expected:
--- Book 1 ---
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Author: Douglas Adams
ISBN: 978-0345391803
--- End Book 1 ---
--- Book 2 ---
Title: 1984
Author: George Orwell
ISBN: 978-0451524935
--- End Book 2 ---
--- Book 3 ---
Title: 
Author: Unknown Author
ISBN: 
--- End Book 3 ---

errors: The `displayBookInfo` method does not explicitly check the state of `std::cout` before performing output operations. In environments where the output stream might fail (e.g., full disk, broken pipe), this can lead to silent failures or undefined behavior.

Using `std::endl` on every output line within `displayBookInfo` forces a flush of the output buffer each time. While not incorrect, it can be less efficient than using `\n` and flushing less frequently (e.g., once at the end of the method or relying on `std::cout`'s default buffering behavior), potentially impacting performance in high-throughput scenarios.
